danger_signals.signal: Dark Elf Wizard Pazort and followers
danger_signals.visual_form: Dark-themed character sprites with implied arcane/shadow aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonistic force; visual differentiation from hero Mars through darker coloration and design language

danger_signals.visual_form: Large-scale entity manifestation representing catastrophic threat
danger_signals.meaning: World-ending danger state; existential threat to Odegan kingdom

danger_signals.visual_form: Princess character in distressed/controlled state at Odegan Castle
danger_signals.meaning: Leverage point for antagonists; signals vulnerability of royal lineage and kingdom stability

safety_signals.visual_form: Fortified castle structure as player starting location
safety_signals.meaning: Sanctuary and institutional authority; Mars employed here as squire under paternal legacy

safety_signals.visual_form: Magical artifact object held by legitimate monarch
safety_signals.meaning: Source of protective power; kingdom's defense mechanism when secured

safety_signals.visual_form: Allied characters from previous Shining Force II continuity
safety_signals.meaning: External support network; established heroes providing legitimacy to player's cause

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: King Odegan's castle and royal court
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Odegan Castle as central institutional hub; kingdom lands of Parmecia/Palacia (regional name variant)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dark Elf wizard identity with arcane affiliations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy sprites differentiated through dark theming; visual opposition to Mars's hero archetype
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Locations corrupted or controlled by Pazort's schemes; spaces connected to Giant summoning ritual

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Zeon identity as predecessor dark force (Zhaion in NA localization)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Pazort established as former Zeon follower; visual connection to prior Shining series conflicts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: References in Japanese/EU versions to Zeon's lingering influence; Sarah and Kazin actively hunting remaining henchmen

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Odegan Castle standing intact; Mars employed as squire; Princess Satera present at court
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Normal castle operations; peaceful kingdom administration; no active threat manifestation

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Deception and conspiracy (Mars's false claims phase)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mars's squire status established through lies about paternal legacy; court environments appear normal but contain hidden threats
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mars unknowingly positioned as obstacle to Pazort's schemes; false reputation creates critical vulnerability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Princess Satera coerced/captured; ritual site preparation for Giant summoning; dark elf wizard presence at castle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pazort's followers actively pursuing orb acquisition; kingdom defense mechanisms threatened; urgent quest activation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Giant Seeega manifestation threat; world-ending catastrophe visualization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All kingdom forces mobilized; Sarah and Kazin intervene from external continuity; Mars must redeem his failures

environmental_storytelling.example: Odegan Castle serves as symbol of legitimate authority and sanctuary; Mars's employment there establishes his initial social position and creates ironic tension with his false claims
Princess Satera's presence at castle represents both kingdom continuity and vulnerability; her capture signals cascade of consequences from Mars's deceptions
Sarah and Kazin's appearance establishes connection to Shining Force II events; Zeon references (in JP/EU versions) create historical depth of antagonistic forces across game timeline
King Odegan's orb functions as protective talisman and target objective; its security status determines kingdom survival outcome
NA version name changes (Kazin→Parn, Sarah→Salah, Parmecia→Palacia) obscure series continuity; EU/JP versions maintain visual-narrative connections through preserved nomenclature

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Operating under fabricated paternal legacy claims; appears as legitimate squire while harboring internal failures
Transitions from deceiver to antagonist obstacle; must undertake redemptive action against Pazort's schemes
Actively pursuing Princess Satera coercion and orb acquisition; exploiting Mars's false reputation as indirect tool
Becomes leverage point; transition from court presence to captive/controlled state indicates escalation of antagonistic threat
Roaming continent hunting Zeon remnants; provide external validation and combat support against Pazort despite Mars being primary protagonist
Represents latent catastrophic power; behavioral manifestation deferred until summoning completion would trigger world-ending scenario

notes: Shining Wisdom employs visual-narrative systems typical of Zelda-style action-adventure design with 2D sprites and top-down perspective. Faction identity relies heavily on character sprite differentiation and castle/environmental architecture rather than explicit color-coding. World-state communication depends on character placement, princess status changes, and antagonist presence escalation. Critical gap: source material provides minimal detail on actual visual design specifics (sprite coloration, enemy variants, environmental corruption effects). Localization variants (Working Designs NA vs. Sega Europe/Japan) significantly impact faction continuity visibility—NA version deliberately obscures series legacy connections through name changes, while JP/EU maintain antagonistic historical context (Zeon continuity). The game's visual grammar emphasizes institutional authority (castle) vs. magical threat (dark elf, giant summoning) dichotomy. Behavioral tells primarily driven by character state transitions and objective markers rather than documented environmental AI.
